source_workbook_filename: 廣東話句子自然度評估 — 評審 B (Responses).xlsx

exception.reason: one-time exception preserves ten complete same-contrast responses while retaining the missing explicit native-language and anonymous-use consent fields as permanent limitations

limitations: binary scale collapses fully natural and merely acceptable judgments
fixed order and no unrelated fillers may create response-strategy and order effects
context-free presentation affects objectless, experiential, placement, and bare-VO judgments
native Cantonese status was inferred from user recruitment and the completed Cantonese background field rather than an explicit yes/no question
anonymous-use consent was not explicitly collected in the legacy form
naturalness judgments do not by themselves validate the parser's structural analysis

items.evidence_note: 8/10 accepted; supports simple declarative V–咗–overt object, with sentence-final-particle preferences visible in comments.
9/10 accepted; supports simple declarative V–咗–overt object.
9/10 accepted; supports an overt quantified NP object, though one respondent requested more discourse context.
9/10 accepted; supports an overt numeral-classifier object; comments prefer classifier omission in some styles.
8/10 accepted; supports an overt associative NP object, with politeness/discourse comments not structural rejection.
5/10 accepted; the brand noun and final-question wording produce a split result, so this item is excluded from the narrow core claim.
5/10 accepted; objectless V–咗 is context-sensitive and belongs to the broader PerfectiveVP route, not this overt-object subtype.
0/10 accepted in isolation; speakers consistently preferred either V完O or V咗O. This remains a distinct CompletionVP profile.
0/10 accepted in this context-free form despite known contextual uses; useful only as a structural 過-versus-咗 boundary, not as evidence that the sentence is generally ungrammatical.
10/10 accepted; confirms that a natural progressive string remains a separate construction rather than a negative grammaticality control.
9/10 accepted; confirms that a natural motion-goal perfective is separate from the overt-object subtype.
4/10 accepted; comments dispute 擺 with a wall location. It remains a placement-profile boundary, not a clean grammaticality control.
0/10 accepted; supports the separate semantic incompatibility guard. The structural V–咗–NP span may still be preserved under NeedsContext.
6/10 accepted; context-sensitive bare VO remains separate from the perfective subtype.
